Documentation.Summary: A component with this interface will not run on dedicated servers.
A component with this interface will run in the editor.

ConstructorArguments: Called when this collider/rigidbody starts touching another collider.

Documentation.Summary: Called when this collider/rigidbody starts touching another collider.

ConstructorArguments: Called once per physics step for every collider being touched.

Documentation.Summary: Called once per physics step for every collider being touched.

ConstructorArguments: Called when this collider/rigidbody stops touching another collider.

Documentation.Summary: Called when this collider/rigidbody stops touching another collider.
A <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" /> with this interface can react to collisions.
When applied to a <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" />, the component will be able to provide the color to use for certain UI editor elements.
A component that can be damaged by something.
Returns bounds, in local space
A component that has bounds

ConstructorArguments: Called on the host to decide whether to accept a Connection. If any Component that implements this returns false, the connection will be denied.

Documentation.Summary:
Called on the host to decide whether to accept a <see cref="T:Sandbox.Connection" />. If any <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" />
that implements this returns false, the connection will be denied.

Params.reason: The reason to display to the client.

ConstructorArguments: Called when someone joins the server. This will only be called for the host.

Documentation.Summary: Called when someone joins the server. This will only be called for the host.

ConstructorArguments: Called when someone leaves the server. This will only be called for the host.

Documentation.Summary: Called when someone leaves the server. This will only be called for the host.

ConstructorArguments: Called when someone is all loaded and entered the game. This will only be called for the host.

Documentation.Summary: Called when someone is all loaded and entered the game. This will only be called for the host.

ConstructorArguments: Called when the host of the game has left - and you are now the new host.

Documentation.Summary: Called when the host of the game has left - and you are now the new host.
A <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" /> with this interface can react to network events.
Read data from the snapshot.
Write data to the snapshot.

When implemented on a <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" /> or <see cref="T:Sandbox.GameObjectSystem" /> it can read and write
data to and from a network snapshot.

ConstructorArguments: Called when this object is spawned on the network.

Documentation.Summary: Called when this object is spawned on the network.

A <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" /> with this interface can listen for when a GameObject
in its ancestors has been network spawned.

ConstructorArguments: Whether this networked object is visible for the specified Connection.

Documentation.Summary:
A <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" /> with this interface can determine whether a networked object
should be visible for a specific <see cref="T:Sandbox.Connection" />.

ConstructorArguments: A player has started looking at this

Documentation.Summary: A player has started looking at this

ConstructorArguments: A player is still looking at this. Called every frame.

ConstructorArguments: A player has stopped looking at this

ConstructorArguments: Pressed. Returns true on success, else false. If it returns true then you should call Release when the press finishes. Not everything expects it, but some stuff will.

Documentation.Summary:
Pressed. Returns true on success, else false.
If it returns true then you should call Release when the
press finishes. Not everything expects it, but some stuff will.

ConstructorArguments: Still being pressed. Return true to allow the press to continue, false cancel the press

Documentation.Summary: Still being pressed. Return true to allow the press to continue, false cancel the press

ConstructorArguments: To be called when the press finishes. You should only call this after a successful press - ie when Press hass returned true.

Documentation.Summary:
To be called when the press finishes. You should only call this
after a successful press - ie when Press hass returned true.

ConstructorArguments: Return true if the press is possible right now

Documentation.Summary: Return true if the press is possible right now

ConstructorArguments: Get a tooltip to show when looking at this pressable

Documentation.Summary: Get a tooltip to show when looking at this pressable

A component that can be pressed. Like a button. This could be by 
a player USE'ing it, or by a player walking on it, or by an NPC.
A call to Press should ALWAYS call release afterwards. Generally
this is done by the player, where holding E presses the button, and
releasing E stops pressing it. You need to handle edge cases where
the player dies while holding etc.
Describes who pressed it.
A tooltip to show when looking at this pressable
A tooltip to show when looking at this pressable
Called on update start. This is the very first thing called.

ConstructorArguments: Indicates to the target object that we want it to die. If it's looping then it should stop now and put itself in a state where it will eventually die.

Documentation.Summary:
Indicates to the target object that we want it to die. If it's looping then
it should stop now and put itself in a state where it will eventually die.

ConstructorArguments: Disable the any looping effects. This indicates to the target object that we want it to die soon.

Documentation.Summary: Disable the any looping effects. This indicates to the target object that we want it to die soon.

ConstructorArguments: Should return true if the effect is active in a visible way

Documentation.Summary: Should return true if the effect is active in a visible way

Allows components to indicate their state in a generic way. This is useful if you have a temporary effect system in which
you want to remove GameObjects when their effects have all finished.
A <see cref="T:Sandbox.Component" /> that lets you change its color.
